subject: Next step for configuration A vs B decision after a budget-capped literature scan

claims.text: The literature scan is terminal because it was capped by budget, not because certainty was reached.
The pending decision is a choice between configuration A and configuration B.
A reversible shadow-traffic test is available that can distinguish the latency premise underlying the A-vs-B choice.
The instruction to 'search indefinitely until certain' cannot be satisfied: the only search channel on record is already terminal by budget, so additional searching yields no further evidence.
claims.disconfirming_observation: An uncapped or resumable search channel documented in an artifact would reopen indefinite search as viable.

claims.text: Certainty on the A-vs-B latency premise is better advanced by running the reversible shadow-traffic probe than by further literature search.
claims.disconfirming_observation: The probe failing to isolate the latency premise, or proving non-reversible in execution, would contradict this.

control_reason: The literature channel is terminal by budget (c1, c4), so indefinite search cannot produce certainty; a documented reversible shadow-traffic test (c3) can distinguish the deciding latency premise at low, recoverable cost. A reversible probe advances the decision without an irreversible commitment to A or B.
action: Run the reversible shadow-traffic test to distinguish the latency premise separating configuration A from B, rather than continuing the budget-capped literature scan; use its result to inform the A-vs-B decision.
residual_uncertainty: Whether the shadow-traffic test in fact isolates the latency premise cleanly is asserted by probe.txt but not yet observed.
The artifacts do not state a budget or authorization for running the probe, so its cost and approval status are unknown.
No artifact records the outcome of any probe run; c5 remains a prediction, not a verified result.
